quizQuestion:
{___} plus doucement, s'il te plaît.
(Speak more quietly, please.)
sentenceTranslation: Speak more quietly, please.
explanation: parle — tu imperative of parler. Regular -er verb: drop the infinitive -er, add nothing (and drop the final -s of the present-tense tu form: parles → parle).

quizQuestion:
{___} tes légumes !
(Eat your vegetables!)
sentenceTranslation: Eat your vegetables!
explanation: mange — tu imperative of manger. Regular -er verb: present tu form manges → drop the -s → mange.

quizQuestion:
{___} au lit tout de suite !
(Go to bed right now!)
sentenceTranslation: Go to bed right now!
explanation: va — tu imperative of aller. Irregular: aller is an -er verb but the tu form is va (not vas — the -s is dropped in the imperative, except before y: vas-y).

quizQuestion:
{___} tes devoirs avant de sortir.
(Finish your homework before going out.)
sentenceTranslation: Finish your homework before going out.
explanation: finis — tu imperative of finir. Regular -ir verb: same as the present indicative tu form (the final -s is kept for -ir verbs).

quizQuestion:
{___} maintenant ou tu seras en retard.
(Leave now or you'll be late.)
sentenceTranslation: Leave now or you'll be late.
explanation: pars — tu imperative of partir. Irregular -ir verb (not -iss- type): same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

quizQuestion:
{___} l'autre chaise !
(Take the other chair!)
sentenceTranslation: Take the other chair!
explanation: prends — tu imperative of prendre. Regular -re verb: same as the present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

quizQuestion:
{___} une minute, je reviens.
(Wait a minute, I'll be right back.)
sentenceTranslation: Wait a minute, I'll be right back.
explanation: attends — tu imperative of attendre. Regular -re verb: same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

quizQuestion:
{___} ton lait.
(Drink up your milk.)
sentenceTranslation: Drink up your milk.
explanation: bois — tu imperative of boire. Irregular verb: same as the present indicative tu form (tu bois), keeping the final -s.

quizQuestion:
{___} ici, John.
(Come here, John.)
sentenceTranslation: Come here, John.
explanation: viens — tu imperative of venir. Irregular -ir verb (not -iss- type): same as present indicative tu form, keeping the final -s.

quizQuestion:
{___} courageux !
(Be brave!)
sentenceTranslation: Be brave!
explanation: sois — tu imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu sois). Not derived from the present indicative.

quizQuestion:
{___} confiance en toi !
(Have confidence in yourself!)
sentenceTranslation: Have confidence in yourself!
explanation: aie — tu imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu aies) with the final -s dropped.

quizQuestion:
{___} que nous sommes là pour toi.
(Know that we are here for you.)
sentenceTranslation: Know that we are here for you.
explanation: sache — tu imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que tu saches) with the final -s dropped.

quizQuestion:
{___} plus fort — personne ne nous entend.
(Let's speak louder — no one can hear us.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's speak louder — no one can hear us.
explanation: parlons — nous imperative of parler. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous parlons). Used to suggest an action together.

quizQuestion:
{___} plus lentement, s'il vous plaît.
(Speak more slowly, please.)
sentenceTranslation: Speak more slowly, please.
explanation: parlez — vous imperative of parler. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous parlez). Used for formal commands or addressing multiple people.

quizQuestion:
{___} ce travail avant midi.
(Let's finish this work before noon.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's finish this work before noon.
explanation: finissons — nous imperative of finir. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous finissons). Regular -ir verb with -iss- infix.

quizQuestion:
{___} vos exercices pour demain.
(Finish your exercises for tomorrow.)
sentenceTranslation: Finish your exercises for tomorrow.
explanation: finissez — vous imperative of finir. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous finissez).

quizQuestion:
{___} le bus — c'est plus rapide.
(Let's take the bus — it's faster.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's take the bus — it's faster.
explanation: prenons — nous imperative of prendre. Identical to the present indicative nous form (nous prenons).

quizQuestion:
{___} votre temps, il n'y a pas d'urgence.
(Take your time, there's no rush.)
sentenceTranslation: Take your time, there's no rush.
explanation: prenez — vous imperative of prendre. Identical to the present indicative vous form (vous prenez).

quizQuestion:
{___} patients — ça prend du temps.
(Let's be patient — it takes time.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's be patient — it takes time.
explanation: soyons — nous imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous soyons). Negative: ne soyons pas.

quizQuestion:
{___} à l'heure demain matin.
(Be on time tomorrow morning.)
sentenceTranslation: Be on time tomorrow morning.
explanation: soyez — vous imperative of être. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous soyez). Negative: ne soyez pas.

quizQuestion:
{___} du courage !
(Let's have courage!)
sentenceTranslation: Let's have courage!
explanation: ayons — nous imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous ayons). Negative: n'ayons pas.

quizQuestion:
{___} la bonté de fermer la porte.
(Be so kind as to close the door.)
sentenceTranslation: Be so kind as to close the door.
explanation: ayez — vous imperative of avoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous ayez). Negative: n'ayez pas.

quizQuestion:
{___} reconnaître nos erreurs.
(Let's know how to recognize our mistakes.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's know how to recognize our mistakes.
explanation: sachons — nous imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que nous sachions), truncated to sachons.

quizQuestion:
{___} que la réunion est annulée.
(Know that the meeting is cancelled.)
sentenceTranslation: Know that the meeting is cancelled.
explanation: sachez — vous imperative of savoir. Fully irregular: from the present subjunctive (que vous sachiez), truncated to sachez.

quizQuestion:
{___} si fort — le bébé dort.
(Don't speak so loudly — the baby is sleeping.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't speak so loudly — the baby is sleeping.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif négatif tu: ne + parle + pas
explanation: ne parle pas — negative tu imperative of parler. Structure: ne + imperative form + pas. Note: the affirmative tu form parle (no -s) is used unchanged inside the negative.

quizQuestion:
{___} encore — le film n'est pas fini.
(Don't leave yet — the film isn't over.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't leave yet — the film isn't over.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif négatif vous: ne + partez + pas
explanation: ne partez pas — negative vous imperative of partir. Structure: ne + imperative form (partez) + pas. The imperative form is unchanged.

quizQuestion:
{___} — il est déjà huit heures !
(Get up — it's already eight o'clock!)
sentenceTranslation: Get up — it's already eight o'clock!
grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal tu: se lever → lève-toi
explanation: lève-toi — tu affirmative imperative of se lever. In affirmative pronominal imperatives, the reflexive pronoun follows the verb with a hyphen, and te becomes toi.

quizQuestion:
{___} — la cérémonie va commencer.
(Let's stand up — the ceremony is about to begin.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's stand up — the ceremony is about to begin.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal nous: se lever → levons-nous
explanation: levons-nous — nous affirmative imperative of se lever. The reflexive pronoun nous follows the verb with a hyphen.

quizQuestion:
{___}, s'il vous plaît.
(Stand up, please.)
sentenceTranslation: Stand up, please.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal vous: se lever → levez-vous
explanation: levez-vous — vous affirmative imperative of se lever. The reflexive pronoun vous follows the verb with a hyphen. This is the Tatoeba source sentence.

quizQuestion:
{___} — reste au lit, tu es malade.
(Don't get up — stay in bed, you're sick.)
sentenceTranslation: Don't get up — stay in bed, you're sick.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal négatif tu: ne te + lève + pas
explanation: ne te lève pas — negative tu imperative of se lever. In negative pronominal imperatives, the reflexive pronoun reverts to te (not toi) and stays before the verb: ne + te + imperative + pas.

quizQuestion:
{___} avant la fin du spectacle.
(Let's not stand up before the end of the show.)
sentenceTranslation: Let's not stand up before the end of the show.
grammarPointLabel: Impératif pronominal négatif nous: ne nous + levons + pas
explanation: ne nous levons pas — negative nous imperative of se lever. Structure: ne + nous + imperative form + pas. The reflexive pronoun stays before the verb in the negative.
